title: Alternative Education
metaDescription: Rosehill College leads the Alternative Education programme for Papakura, offering flexible learning pathways for students who need them.

sections.heading: Alternative Education Programme
sections.lede: Rosehill College leads the Alternative Education (Alt Ed) Programme for the Papakura area, in partnership with Blue Light Papakura and Te Awatea.

sections.heading: About the programme
sections.html:
The programme provides flexible, supportive learning pathways for students in Years 9–11 (under 16 years old) who need a tailored environment to succeed. Its purpose is to provide new learning opportunities for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education.

This programme is designed for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education and would benefit from a more personalised and supportive learning environment.

items.text: Contact the Alt Ed team or speak with your Kaitiaki.
Complete and return the Alternative Education Enrolment Form.
Student, whānau, and referring school meet with the Alt Ed team.
A personalised learning and attendance plan is developed.

sections.heading: Programme focus

image.alt: Rosehill College staff supporting a student one to one

sections.html:
• Core learning: literacy, numeracy, and NCEA Foundation Level
  • Pathways: transition planning, employment skills, and work readiness
  • Life skills: hauora, resilience, communication, and teamwork
  • Service and leadership: building social responsibility and whanaungatanga
  • Support: small class sizes, mentoring, and wrap-around pastoral care

sections.heading: Programme options (2026)
sections.lede: The offsite programmes offer pick-ups and drop-offs, breakfast and lunch, and access to facilities including a fitness centre, music recording, and outdoor education.
sections.caption: Alternative Education programme options for 2026
rows: Re-engagement, foundation learning, transition to school
Holistic, whānau-based learning and wellbeing support

sections.heading: Expected outcomes
sections.html:
The programme aims to support students to:

  • Re-engage in education
  • Improve attendance and engagement
  • Develop literacy, numeracy, and communication skills
  • Build confidence, resilience, and self-motivation
  • Transition into further education, training, or employment

sections.heading: Downloads
sections.lede: Find out more about the programme in our information guide.

sections.heading: Apply now
sections.text: To apply, please complete the Alternative Education Enrolment Form and return it to the Alt Ed team at alted@rosehillcollege.school.nz.
